[AI]
Great finds for your fridge! I pulled together 3 tasty options that use as many of your ingredients as possible: chicken, broccoli, bell peppers (red and green), lemon, eggs, and maybe yogurt/milk if you want to treat a dish more creamy. All are quick, one-pan or few steps, and kid-friendly.

Quick Picks
- One-Pan Lemon Chicken with Broccoli & Peppers — bright lemon, a simple sear, and crisp-tender veg in one pan. [web:weightwatchers]
- Chicken, Broccoli & Bell Pepper Stir-Fry — fast weeknight staple that tosses everything with a lemony finish. [web:food52]
- Broccoli & Pepper Frittata — a veggie-packed breakfast-for-dinner using eggs, broccoli, and peppers. [web:forageddish]

Full Recipe: One-Pan Lemon Chicken with Broccoli & Peppers
- Serves: 4 | Time: ~30-40 mins
- Ingredients used:
  - Chicken (breasts or pieces from your whole chicken, about 1.5-2 lb)
  - Broccoli florets
  - Red bell pepper, sliced (and/or green bell pepper)
  - Lemon (juice and a little zest)
  - Olive oil or another cooking oil
  - Salt and pepper
- Steps:
  1) If using a whole chicken, cut into pieces. Pat dry and season with salt and pepper.
  2) In a large skillet, heat 1–2 tbsp oil over medium-high. Sear chicken until golden on both sides and just cooked through.
  3) Add broccoli florets and sliced peppers. Stir-fry 4–6 minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender.
  4) Squeeze lemon juice over the pan and, if you like, a bit of zest for brightness. Toss to coat.
  5) Adjust salt/pepper to taste and serve hot.
- Nutrition snapshot (approx per serving): Calories ~ 420; Protein ~ 35–40 g; Carbs ~ 12–15 g; Fat ~ 18–22 g.
- Source: [web:weightwatchers]

Full Recipe: Chicken, Broccoli & Bell Pepper Stir-Fry
- Serves: 4 | Time: ~25-30 mins
- Ingredients used:
  - Chicken pieces (from your whole chicken, cut into bite-sized chunks)
  - Broccoli florets
  - Red bell pepper (and/or green bell pepper), sliced
  - Olive oil
  - Lemon juice (for brightness)
  - Salt and pepper
- Steps:
  1) Season chicken with salt and pepper.
  2) In a large skillet, heat oil over medium-high and cook chicken until just done.
  3) Add broccoli and peppers; stir-fry 4–6 minutes until still crisp-tender.
  4) Splash with lemon juice; toss, adjust seasoning, and serve.
- Nutrition snapshot (approx per serving): Calories ~ 360-420; Protein ~ 30–35 g; Carbs ~ 12-18 g; Fat ~ 14-20 g.
- Source: [web:food52]

Full Recipe: Broccoli & Pepper Frittata
- Serves: 4 | Time: ~25 mins (plus a quick bake if you like)
- Ingredients used:
  - Eggs (6 large)
  - Broccoli florets
  - Bell peppers (any color), diced
  - Olive oil
  - Salt and pepper
  - Optional: a little cheese if you have some
- Steps:
  1) Preheat oven to 375°F (or prepare a skillet with a lid to finish on the stove).
  2) Sauté broccoli and peppers in a bit of oil 5–7 minutes until tender.
  3) Whisk eggs with salt and pepper. Pour over veggies in the pan.
  4) Cook on the stovetop until the edges set, then finish under the broiler or in the oven until the center is set (about 6–10 minutes).
  5) Slice and serve.
- Nutrition snapshot (approx per serving): Calories ~ 180-220; Protein ~ 12–16 g; Carbs ~ 6-8 g; Fat ~ 12-14 g.
- Source: [web:forageddish]

Tips and swaps
- Make-ahead: Prep all veg (peppers and broccoli) in advance and store in the fridge. Chicken can be cut and seasoned the night before.
- Pantry-friendly tweaks: If you have yogurt you’d like to use for a creamy touch, mix a spoonful into the lemon juice for a light dressing to toss with the stir-fry, or whisk into eggs for a silkier frittata. If you want extra brightness, finish with a quick squeeze of lemon on each plate.
- Storage: Refrigerate leftovers in airtight containers for up to 3 days. Reheat gently to avoid overcooking the broccoli.
- Substitutions: If you’re short on lemon, a splash of any citrus you have works too. Any color pepper works; you can use all broccoli or mix with other veggies you have.

Which to try first? Want me to tailor one of these to your serving size or prefer a different combination (e.g., add yogurt-based flavor, or a different veggie from your fridge)? If you’d like, I can also draft a simple shopping list for any missing pieces. More ingredients in your fridge? I can remix these into more options.

Yes—you can absolutely add a bright lemon-yogurt sauce to the One-Pan Lemon Chicken with Broccoli & Peppers. Here are three quick, fridge-friendly options that use yogurt and lemon (plus a few optional extras if you have them).

Sauce Option 1: Quick Lemon Yogurt Pan Sauce
- What it is: A bright, creamy glaze you stir into the pan at the end.
- Ingredients (from your fridge): yogurt, lemon juice, lemon zest (optional), salt, pepper, olive oil.
- How to do it:
  1) After the chicken and veg are cooked, remove them briefly from the pan.
  2) In a small bowl, whisk together 1/2 cup yogurt, 1–2 tbsp lemon juice, 1/2 tsp lemon zest (if you have it), 1 tsp olive oil, salt and pepper to taste.
  3) Return pan contents to the pan, reduce heat to low, and whisk in the yogurt mixture just until glossy and warmed through (don’t boil).
- Source idea: Easy lemon yogurt sauce concept from The Recipe Well. [web:the_recipe_well]

Sauce Option 2: Lemon-Garlic Yogurt Sauce
- What it is: A simple, savory glaze with garlic brightness.
- Ingredients: yogurt, lemon juice, garlic (1 small clove minced or 1/4 tsp garlic powder), salt, pepper, olive oil.
- How to do it:
  1) Sauté or briefly bloom the garlic in a little oil in the pan.
  2) Stir in 1/2 cup yogurt and 1–2 tbsp lemon juice; season with salt and pepper.
  3) Pour over the chicken and veg in the pan for a quick coat.
- Source idea: Lemon garlic yogurt marinated chicken vibe (uses yogurt, lemon, and garlic). [web:hungryhappens]

Sauce Option 3: Dill-Lemon Yogurt Sauce
- What it is: A fresh, herby finish that pairs nicely with peppers and broccoli.
- Ingredients: yogurt, lemon juice, fresh dill (or dried), salt, pepper, a tiny drizzle of olive oil.
- How to do it:
  1) Mix 1/2 cup yogurt with 1–2 tbsp lemon juice and 1–2 tsp chopped dill (or 1/2 tsp dried dill).
  2) Season with salt and pepper, then swirl into the pan off the heat or drizzle on top after plating.
- Source idea: Lemon, dill & yogurt marinade inspiration (yogurt + lemon + herbs). [web:greenvalleylactosefree]

Tips
- If you don’t have fresh dill or garlic, Option 1 still works perfectly on its own.
- Want it creamier? Use Greek yogurt (thicker) or whisk in a splash of milk to reach your preferred consistency.
- Want extra brightness? A pinch of lemon zest at the end really pops.

Would you like me to tailor one of these to your exact amounts (how many servings you’re making, and how saucy you want it)? Also—if you have any other ingredients in the fridge (cheese, herbs, etc.), I can tweak the sauce to use them too.
